author: @FinnOtaku

text:
TheCheezWizz ﻿
Yes, when people say can-can these days, they usually refer to this particular piece of music. But, unlike Galop Infernal, can-can also refers to a form of dance, Galop Infernal being one of the most prevalent songs of that genre. Calling the song Can-can in this context would be like calling the 14. song of this video McRoll'd; popular nickname, but not it's real title. I just corrected Shpee's apparent misconception of the song's true title.
Now, if I were to be as sophisticated as you, I would end this by calling you an uneducated fuck, but since you might already know what I just wrote here, and just tried to raise my awareness on this nickname, I'll just leave it at that.

author: @KhazWolf

text:
YouTube Poop MeDley. And I believe you meant related YTPMVs.
The whole concept basically is: A remixed medley, featuring songs familiar from various YTPMV fads/videos
author: @FinnOtaku

text: YTPMD stands for YouTube Poop Medley. YTPMVs are YouTube Poop Music Videos which are compilation of a character saying something with the pitch and speed being changed to make it sound like a popular internet song. This is like that but with no voices,only one instrument playing all the songs.
